passive.name: Mechanical Minions
passive.description:
Every time you defeat an enemy, a robot will be automatically summoned to attack the enemy for 6 seconds. Robots that are not defeated by the end of the duration will combine with nearby robots of the same level and level up (maximum Lv.3). If there are no robots nearby that can be combined, the robot will self-destruct, dealing 200 (200 + 30% of magic attack) magic damage to nearby enemies (blast radius increases depending on robot level).
Robot normal attack: 120 (120 + 25% of magic attack) magic damage, HP: 500 (500 + 110% of magic attack), Movement speed: 450 (450 + 10% of magic attack).
Damage from tower/crystal attacks is reduced by 75%. Each time they combine, normal attack and self-destruct damage increases by 30%, and maximum HP increases by 20%.
Up to 4 (Lv.1)/2 (Lv.2)/1 (Lv.3) robots can exist at the same time. When the upper limit is exceeded, the robots summoned first will combine/self-destruct.

skill1.name: Air Superiority
skill1.description:
Launch a fighter jet in the specified direction and perform multiple attacks on the enemy closest to you along the route. Each attack deals 65 (65 + 10% magic attack) magic damage, and every second attack deals 140 (140 + 20% magic attack) magic damage to the nearest enemy.
If you activate skill 1 again while the fighter plane is in flight, it will transform into two small fighter planes, each of which will perform multiple attacks on the nearest enemy on its route. Each attack deals 65 (65 + 10% of magic attack) magic damage. When an enemy is attacked by two small fighters at the same time, their movement speed is reduced by 25% for 1.5 seconds.

skill2.name: Forced Invasion
skill2.description:
Drops two robots at the specified location and deals 100 (100 + 20% of magic attack) magic damage to surrounding enemies when they land. The robot will start attacking nearby enemies after 1 second.
Stack Skill 2 once every 15 to 12 seconds (subject to cooldown reduction). Up to 3 stacks.

skill3.name: Chaos Field
skill3.description:
Fire a signal device at a target or enemy tower/crystal to create an imprint that lasts 3 seconds and stuns non-tower units for 0.75 seconds. While inscribed, deals 80 (80 + 4% of magic attack) magic damage every 0.5 seconds and reduces enemy movement speed by 15%. The engraving will explode when the duration expires, dealing magic damage to the target and surrounding enemies equal to 25% of the damage dealt to the target while the engraving was applied.
If you defeat the target before the engraving disappears, 3 robots will be summoned. When activated, allied robots within range will have their attack speed increased by 100% and their movement speed increased by 100%, and will immediately begin attacking the target.
The stun effect of the signal device has no effect on towers/crystals.

strengths: Overwhelming lane/tower push power using robots (minions)
Control ability that applies pressure from the beginning and keeps opponents in the lane.
weaknesses: Low mobility and very vulnerable to Assassin ganks and close quarters combat.
Against heroes who can quickly deal with robots, it is easy to completely block their strengths.
strategy.earlyGame: Early Game Strategy: Summon mechanical minions (robots) in the Mid Lane to push the lane hard, forcing enemies under their tower. When the enemy roams away, quickly use your minions to wear down the tower's HP.
strategy.midGame: Mid Game Strategy: Keep control of your lane while assisting sidelanes in taking down towers. Robots can be used to grant vision or block enemy skills, so always deploy them to keep up the pressure.
strategy.lateGame: Late Game Strategy: Rather than teamfights, finding opportunities for split pushing (destroying towers alone) is extremely powerful. However, since you are easily targeted by Assassins when isolated, carefully track enemy positions on the minimap.
strategy.teamfight: Teamfight Strategy: Deploy multiple robots to scatter enemy aggro while hitting the enemy carry or diving Assassins with your Ultimate to stun them. Maintain a safe position from the backline.
strategy.commonMistakes: Common Beginner Mistakes: Pushing the lane too deep alone and getting caught despite having low mobility and only your Ultimate for self-defense. Neglecting robot management, resulting in low damage when needed.

synergy.reason: This is because the movement speed buff compensates for the lack of mobility and allows for smooth tower siege by deploying robots.
This is because it prevents enemies from approaching and creates an environment where Milady can safely summon robots and use her ultimate.

counters.reason: They ignore robots and quickly approach them with high mobility, and are instantly defeated by powerful burst damage.
With a wide range of skills, the robot army that he had worked so hard to summon could be easily wiped out.

passive.name: Flow of the World
passive.description:
Use the base to bring the flow into your body. Attack speed increases by 10% to 20% (increases according to hero level), and can be further strengthened by choosing one of the following four types.
Vitality: Maximum HP increases by 300.
Penetration: Increases physical attack by 30.
Swift: Increases movement speed by 3.5%.
Focus: Reduces cooldown by 5%.
Enhanced normal attack: Fires a piercing arrow, dealing 80 (80 + 20% of additional physical attack) physical damage to enemies along the route (hitting an enemy increases flight distance, but reduces damage by 30%).
You can activate a strengthened normal attack by consuming one stack of "Flow".

skill1.name: Convergence
skill1.description:
Collects "flow", deals 100 (100 + 25% of additional physical attack) physical damage to nearby enemies, knocks them back (no CC), and recovers own HP by 150 (150 + 4% of additional maximum HP). At the same time, acquire 5 stacks of ``Flow'' and reduce the cooldown of ``Arrow of the Cauldron'' and ``Storm of Many Arrows'' by 1/2.
Gain additional healing effects (up to 100%) and stacks of Flow (up to 9 stacks) depending on the number of friendly heroes and minions nearby.

skill2.name: Arrow of No Retreat
skill2.description:
Fires a giant arrow at the target (stops when it hits an enemy hero), dealing 450 (450 + 120% of additional physical attack) physical damage and knocking back non-hero units. After activation, you can move a certain distance, and if activated repeatedly within 1.5 seconds, you will gain a strengthening effect.
When activated twice in a row: Arrow damage increases by 25%.
When activated 3 times in a row: Becomes an air attack and arrow damage is increased by 50%.
By consuming 4 stacks of ``Flow'', you can additionally activate ``Arrow of the Cauldron''.
Activation speed increases according to attack speed.

skill3.name: Arrowstorm
skill3.description:
Fires 7 small arrows (activation speed increases according to attack speed) towards the specified area, deals 50 (50 + 24% of physical attack) physical damage (3 arrows are considered 1 normal attack) to the specified area (locks on to the target within the area), and increases own movement speed by 10% (if activated again, it becomes an air attack).
You can additionally activate "Storm of Many Arrows" by consuming 5 stacks of "Flow".

strengths: Since you can change classes, it is easy to avoid opponent's counters during the draft stage.
Customizable abilities allow you to increase attack speed and firepower to suit the situation.
weaknesses: Compared to specialized marksmen, it is slightly inferior in terms of pure firepower and survivability.
If you are not familiar with the operational methods and meta of each role, it will be difficult to demonstrate your true strengths.
strategy.earlyGame: Early Game Strategy: In the Farm Lane, utilize your passive's enhanced basic attack (Piercing Arrow) to simultaneously clear minions and harass the enemy. Take advantage of your long range to farm safely.
strategy.midGame: Mid Game Strategy: Once you start getting your core items, utilize Skill 2's movement and damage boost to engage in highly mobile skirmishes. Coordinate with your Roamer to roam the map and provide sustained damage during objective fights.
strategy.lateGame: Late Game Strategy: Serve as your team's main source of damage. Manage your 'Flow' stacks, and use Skill 3's (Ultimate) movement speed buff and rapid fire to continuously deal damage without letting enemies close the gap (kiting).
strategy.teamfight: Teamfight Strategy: Always position yourself behind your allies and attack enemies from front to back. If enemy Assassins approach, use Skill 1's knockback or Skill 2's movement to create distance, making fine adjustments to your positioning as you fight.
strategy.commonMistakes: Common Beginner Mistakes: Dashing towards the enemy team with Skill 2, putting yourself in a dangerous position. Ignoring your 'Flow' stacks and missing the timing for your enhanced basic attacks.

synergy.reason: This is because the shield increases the survivability of the rear guard and allows them to be safely raised until the final stage when damage scaling is complete.
His high recovery and protection ability stabilizes lane battles, allowing him to fully utilize his marksman strengths.

counters.reason: With surprise attacks from stealth and high burst damage, you can instantly take down marksmen with low self-defense.
With his overwhelming mobility, he jumped into the rearguard at once and was defeated before he could react.

passive.description: The Benevolent Guardian's normal attacks can penetrate the target and deal 287 (120 + 100% of physical attacks + 35% of magical attacks) magic damage. If the target is a tower/crystal, the damage is reduced to 50%.

skill1.name: Golden Feather Arrow
skill1.description: Deploys a wing for 2.5 seconds, dealing 150 (150 + 30% of magic attack) magic damage every 0.5 seconds and reducing movement speed by 15% for 1 second.

skill2.name: Protection of Golden Wings
skill2.description:
Unleash the power of prayer. Increases movement speed by 100 (decreases continuously) for 3 seconds and gains a shield of 300 (300 + 55% of magic attack). Additionally, deals 200 (200 + 40% of magic attack) magic damage to surrounding enemies.
Passive: Movement speed increases by 15.

skill3.name: Wings of Judgment
skill3.description:
Summons the Golden Feather of Judgment. Fires 90 arrows forward for 5 seconds, dealing 54 (54 + 10% of magic attack) magic damage per arrow and increasing movement speed by 20%. Other skills can be activated while activated.
If activated again while the skill is active, it will immediately release unfired arrows around you, dealing the same amount of magic damage, frightening nearby enemies for 2 seconds, and nullifying the physical attack bonuses from the target's equipment.

strategy.midGame: Mid Game Strategy: Avoid forcing fights until your core items (especially critical strike items) are complete. Rack up assists from behind your allies utilizing your long range, and gather gold to prepare for the late game.

weaknesses: Due to low mobility and short range of skills, it is difficult to fight in group battles.
There is a big gap after all the skills are used, and the wave clearing ability is low.

strategy.midGame: Mid Game Strategy: Once you get your core items, you can one-shot squishy enemies (Mages and Marksmen) with a single combo. Hide in the brush to ambush enemies, aiming for the Skill 2 (Stun) -> Ultimate -> Skill 1 combo.
strategy.lateGame: Late Game Strategy: Your one-shot combo damage becomes devastating, allowing you to instantly burst down enemy carries. In teamfights, instead of fighting front-to-back, play like an Assassin by flanking or ambushing the enemy backline from the brush.
strategy.teamfight: Teamfight Strategy: Be careful not to waste your skills on enemy Tanks, and wait until the enemy Marksman or Mage is within range. After unloading all your skills, temporarily retreat to a safe position until your cooldowns refresh.
strategy.commonMistakes: Common Beginner Mistakes: Casting your Ultimate when its fox fires will split among multiple enemies and minions, failing to burst down a single target. Walking straight up to enemies openly and getting killed before you can land your combo.

This is because before you can fire the skill, you will be attacked by stealth and Daki, who has no durability, will die instantly.

skill3.name: Air Support

strengths: Overwhelming firepower in the late game and damage scaling that can cut through tanks.
Powerful range damage from a long distance due to passive, and excellent visibility ability due to skill.
weaknesses: Since they do not have movement skills, their mobility is low and they are easily targeted by assassins.
His combat power is low in the early stages, and he relies on the protection of his allies until he has enough equipment.
strategy.earlyGame: Early Game Strategy: As you are extremely squishy and lack escape skills, focus entirely on safely farming minions from under your tower. Use your passive's sweeping fire (a technique where you basic attack the air to build stacks) to safely harass enemies from out of range.
strategy.midGame: Mid Game Strategy: Your damage spikes once your core items are completed. Always stick with your Roamer or Tank and avoid being isolated. Use Skill 2's rocket to secure map vision or to finish off low-HP enemies escaping far away.
strategy.lateGame: Late Game Strategy: Thanks to your passive's max HP percentage damage, you become an overwhelming source of damage capable of melting even the tankiest enemies in seconds. It is crucial to constantly track enemy Assassins on the minimap and never leave your allies' protection.
strategy.teamfight: Teamfight Strategy: Position yourself at the very back of the team and continuously unleash your passive sweeping fire after casting skills. If an enemy Assassin closes in, use Skill 2's knockback to create distance, and prioritize surviving by using Flash.
strategy.commonMistakes: Common Beginner Mistakes: Pressing the movement key during your passive's sweeping fire and canceling the attack animation. Farming alone defenselessly without map vision of the enemy.

This is because his ability to maintain his front line is high, and he creates space that makes it easy for him in the back to attack.

counters.reason: Because of their high mobility and burst damage, they can ignore the vanguard and be defeated directly.
Because he has a skill that nullifies physical damage, he is at a disadvantage when trading damage in lane battles.

passive.name: Flame Incantation
passive.description:
Enemies hit by her active skills gain 1 stack of burn effect, and she gains 1 stack of movement speed boost. Each can stack up to 10 times and lasts for 3 seconds.
For each burn stack, enemies take an additional 10 (+2% magical attack) magic damage when damaged by her active skills.
For each movement speed stack, her movement speed is increased by 2%.

skill1.name: Scorching Barrage
skill1.description:
Fires 5 fireballs in the target direction. Each fireball deals 280 (+30% magical attack) magic damage to enemy heroes upon hit and disappears. It also deals equal damage to non-hero units along the path.
If multiple fireballs hit the same enemy hero, the damage of subsequent hits is reduced to 30%.

skill2.name: Chaos Cinder
skill2.description:
Fires Chaos Cinder in the target direction. When the flame hits an enemy or reaches its maximum range, it transforms into a flame vortex, stunning the first enemy hit for 1 second.
While the flame vortex persists, it deals 125 (+20% magical attack) magic damage to nearby enemies every 0.5 seconds and slows their movement speed by 30% for 1.5 seconds.

skill3.name: Blazing Brilliance
skill3.description:
Fires a continuous beam of fire in the target direction. The direction can be adjusted during the cast, dealing 150 (+21% magical attack) magic damage to enemies in its path up to 12 times.
Upon casting, she gains a shield that absorbs 850 (+88% magical attack) damage and grants Control Immunity while the shield persists.
After 0.5 seconds of casting, the skill can be canceled by casting it again.

strengths: Extremely high burst damage capable of melting single targets instantly.
Fast wave clear with skills, and her ultimate provides a shield and Control Immunity for self-protection.
weaknesses: Skill trajectories are easy to predict and can be easily dodged by highly mobile heroes.
Cannot move during her ultimate, making her vulnerable if the shield breaks.
strategy.earlyGame: Early Game Strategy: Use Skill 1 and Skill 2 to quickly clear minions in the Mid Lane, and look for ganks in the sidelanes (especially the Clash Lane). Hiding in the brush to ambush enemies is an extremely powerful move.
strategy.midGame: Mid Game Strategy: Target isolated enemies and rack up kills with the full combo: Skill 2 (Stun) -> Skill 1 -> Ultimate. If you suspect enemies are hiding in areas without vision, cast Skill 1 into the brush to check for safety.
strategy.lateGame: Late Game Strategy: You possess the damage to nearly one-shot even an enemy Tank with a single combo, but you are a stationary target during your Ultimate. You must wait patiently for enemies to use their crowd control (CC) skills before casting your Ultimate.
strategy.teamfight: Teamfight Strategy: Always position yourself slightly behind your Tanks, and unleash your full combo when enemies are grouped up or the moment your allies land a strong CC. Deciding whether to save Skill 2 for self-defense or use it offensively is crucial.
strategy.commonMistakes: Common Beginner Mistakes: Failing to cancel (tap again) your Ultimate when the enemy escapes its range, leaving yourself a stationary and defenseless target. Forcing your Ultimate after missing Skill 2, resulting in insufficient damage.

synergy.reason: Can cleanse CC, making it safer for her to channel her ultimate without being interrupted.
Provides high durability and shields at the frontline, holding enemies in place so she can easily land her skills.

counters.reason: Can quickly close the gap with high mobility and easily assassinate her while she is immobilized during her ultimate.
Can stop her with a targeted hard CC and burst her down instantly.

skill2.name: Honeysweet Breeze

weaknesses: Low durability, making her extremely vulnerable to assassin burst damage and unexpected engages.

strategy.teamfight: Teamfight Strategy: Spam Skill 1 from the backline, and if an enemy Assassin approaches, place Skill 2 at your feet for self-defense. Your Ultimate has a wide range and boosts your movement speed upon hit, making it effective for both chasing and escaping.
strategy.commonMistakes: Common Beginner Mistakes: Wasting your Skill 2 knockup, only to be jumped on by an Assassin while it is on cooldown and getting killed without being able to react. Rushing headfirst into the enemy team to deal damage with your Ultimate.

Can lock down powerful enemy assassins, creating a safe environment for her to deal damage from the backline.

counters.reason: Can close the gap from afar and quickly burst down her fragile health bar before she can combo.
Possesses long-range stuns, allowing him to easily interrupt and poke her from a safe distance.

hero_012.status_text: Zilong Fearless Spear Trainee Assassin 0/100 Assassin/Fighter Diver Mobility Early Game Difficulty: Normal

strengths: Exceptional survivability and durability for an assassin, thanks to high mobility and his damage-reduction and healing abilities.
Excellent at diving into the enemy backline with strong ganking capabilities.
weaknesses: Requires good judgment for timing skill combos and engaging.
Can struggle to deal sufficient damage if he falls behind in gold and experience, leading to a mediocre performance.
strategy.earlyGame: Early Game Strategy: Play as the Jungler and quickly clear your jungle camps to reach Level 4. Once you unlock your Ultimate, your ganks become highly effective, so ambush lanes where enemies are overextended.
strategy.midGame: Mid Game Strategy: Utilize your mobility to roam the map and force skirmishes to secure kills. Hit the enemy carries (Mages and Marksmen) with your Ultimate's knockup followed by the Skill 2 -> Skill 1 combo to control the pace of the game.
strategy.lateGame: Late Game Strategy: Thanks to your passive (damage reduction as your HP decreases), you can fight tenaciously like a Fighter despite being an Assassin. In teamfights, don't engage front-to-back; look to dive the enemy backline directly from the flanking brush.
strategy.teamfight: Teamfight Strategy: Dive the enemy backline with your Ultimate right after your Tank engages or when the enemy's CC skills are on cooldown. Trust your passive and fight to the limit, using Skill 1 to dash over walls and retreat if you're in danger.
strategy.commonMistakes: Common Beginner Mistakes: Randomly casting your Ultimate without predicting the landing spot, missing completely, and ending up isolated in the enemy team without dealing damage or CC. Overestimating your passive at full HP and recklessly initiating a 1vX fight.

synergy.reason: Her massive magic burst damage pairs perfectly with his ultimate knock-up, quickly wiping out enemies.
Provides shields and healing, greatly enhancing his survivability after diving into the enemy formation.

counters.reason: Can completely lock down his mobility with powerful suppression skills, leaving him vulnerable to focused fire.
Uses chain stuns and shields to block his engage and interrupt his attempts to disengage with skills.

passive.name: Fight for Peace
passive.description:
His 4th basic attack is empowered, dealing 450 (+70% magical attack) magic damage to enemies in the area and knocking them back for 0.75 seconds.
Whenever he casts a skill, he gains 1 stack of a shield (up to 3 stacks). Each stack absorbs 300 (+35% magical attack) damage for 3 seconds.

skill1.name: Peacebringer
skill1.description: Pilots his mech to dash in the target direction, dealing 250 (+38% magical attack) magic damage to enemies in his path and empowering his next basic attack.

skill2.name: Cannon Blast
skill2.description:
Pilots his mech to fire an energy shell in the target direction. Upon hitting an enemy or reaching max range, the shell explodes to form a field, dealing 525 (+45% magical attack) magic damage to enemies in the area and stunning them for 1 second.
The field lasts for 4 seconds, dealing 50 (+5% magical attack) magic damage every 0.5 seconds to enemies within.

skill3.name: Think Inside the Box
skill3.description: Pilots his mech to deploy an energy barrier in place that lasts up to 3.3 seconds. Enemies that come into contact with the barrier take 200 (+45% magical attack) magic damage every 0.5 seconds and are stunned.

hero_013.status_text: Mozi Peacekeeper Adjustment Trainee Mage 0/100 Mage Mage/Fighter Poke/Control Balanced Difficulty: Hard

strengths: Exceptional long-range poke and strong hard crowd control abilities.
High survivability for a mage due to his passive shields and dash skill.
weaknesses: Skills require precise aiming, making him highly dependent on the player's accuracy.
His ultimate requires him to be in close proximity to enemies, which can easily be interrupted or expose him to danger.
strategy.earlyGame: Early Game Strategy: Play as the Mid Laner or Roamer. Use your Skill 2's long-range stun to safely clear minions while harassing the enemy. Pay attention to your mana management, as your skills consume a lot of mana.
strategy.midGame: Mid Game Strategy: As you complete your items, Skill 2's damage and cooldown become excellent. Stick with your Jungler or Roamer, and create kill opportunities by landing stuns from the brush where enemies have no vision.
strategy.lateGame: Late Game Strategy: Relentlessly poke the enemy carries (main damage dealers) and focus entirely on whittling down the enemy team's HP before teamfights. Be mindful of firing from the backline and avoid stepping up too far carelessly.
strategy.teamfight: Teamfight Strategy: Poke enemies down with Skill 2, and when your team engages or enemy Fighters/Assassins dive in, use your Ultimate's wide-area stun to lock them down, protecting your allies and turning the tide of battle.
strategy.commonMistakes: Common Beginner Mistakes: Recklessly diving into the enemy team to force your Ultimate, only to get focused and bursted down instantly. Mozi's Ultimate is much more reliable when used for self-defense or counter-engaging.

synergy.reason: Her high burst damage easily eliminates enemies that he locks down with his continuous crowd control.
Provides strong frontline protection, allowing him to safely poke and land his skills from a distance.

counters.reason: His long-range poke and ultimate can easily interrupt his abilities and zone him out.
Her mobility and range make it difficult for him to land his skillshots, allowing her to easily kite him.

passive.name: burst of vitality
passive.description: Each time a normal attack hits an enemy, the CD of Skill 1 is reduced by 0.5 seconds.

skill1.name: Full power assault
skill1.description:
Rolls in the specified direction, strengthens the next normal attack, and deals 454 (285 + 100% of physical attack power) physical damage to enemies along the route. After activating an enhanced normal attack, you can immediately use a normal attack with increased range.
If there is an enemy hero near where you rolled, your movement speed increases by 80% (decreases continuously over 2 seconds).

skill2.name: Crimson bomb
skill2.description:
Throws a bomb at the specified location, dealing 380 (380 + 100% of additional physical attack power) physical damage to enemies within the range and greatly reducing enemy movement speed for 1 second. Additionally, reduces enemy physical defense by 10% for 5 seconds and imprints enemy heroes and minions.
Normal attacks on enemies with the engraving deal an additional 120 (120 + 30% of additional physical attack power) physical damage to heroes and 240 (240 + 60% of additional physical attack power) physical damage to minions.

skill3.name: ultimate crossbow
skill3.description: Fires a ballista in the specified direction, dealing 840 (840 + 210% of additional physical attack power) physical damage to the first enemy hit by the ballista. When the ballista hits an enemy or reaches its maximum distance, it explodes, dealing 75% damage to enemies within a fan-shaped radius from the point of explosion.

hero_014.status_text: Sun Shangxiang Sonshouko Heavy Artillery Princess Adjustment Apprentice Marksman 0/100 Marksman Heavy Artillery Marksman Charge/Kill Endgame Difficulty: Hard

strengths: Overwhelming burst damage and scaling that can melt even the enemy's vanguard in the final stages of the game.
High mobility due to skill 1's rolling and the ability to kite while avoiding enemy skills.
weaknesses: Their combat power is low in the early stages, and they tend to lack firepower until they have the equipment.
Due to its low durability, if you stand in the wrong position or receive CC, you will be defeated in an instant.
strategy.earlyGame: Early Game Strategy: Cooperate with your Support in the Farm Lane, prioritizing safe last-hitting on minions. Since your damage is low early on, use your Skill 1 roll to dodge enemy attacks while prioritizing safe farming from a distance.
strategy.midGame: Mid Game Strategy: Your damage spikes drastically once 1 or 2 core items are completed. Group with your team to siege towers or contest objectives like the Tyrant, using Skill 2 to reduce enemy physical defense before dealing damage.
strategy.lateGame: Late Game Strategy: You become the strongest carry with overwhelming burst damage. Always position yourself behind your Tanks or Supports, making sure not to get isolated. Maintain vision and melt the enemies front-to-back.
strategy.teamfight: Teamfight Strategy: Use Skill 1 to dodge enemy skills while strictly kiting backwards. Be cautious of approaching enemy Assassins and Fighters, and continuously dish out damage with your basic attacks and skills from a safe distance.
strategy.commonMistakes: Common Beginner Mistakes: Rolling forward with Skill 1 into the enemy team and jumping directly into their attack range, resulting in death. Rolling sideways or backwards to maintain a safe distance is the golden rule.

synergy.reason: This is because she can restrain the enemy with strong CC and create an opening for her to inflict burst damage from a safe position.
This is because she can force group battles with a wide range of launches and create an environment where she can move freely and defeat enemies individually.

counters.reason: This is because her mobility will be completely nullified with a guaranteed hard CC, and she will be defeated without giving her a chance to escape.
This is because you can use a long-range stun to stop her movement and prevent her from hitting and away, which is her specialty.

hero_015.status_text: Zhuangzi Sou Dream Sage Apprentice Support 0/100 Support/Tank Guardian Support Team Buff Late Stage Difficulty: Easy

strengths: A powerful anti-CC ability that can release all allies from CC (incapacitated) state.
Excellent survivability due to high damage reduction rate and mobility, and sustained damage output.
weaknesses: Since it does not have hard CC (stun, etc.) that can definitely capture the enemy, it is difficult to initiate battles on its own.
If the enemy team doesn't have a lot of CC or has a lot of percentage damage, it's easy to get corrupted.
strategy.earlyGame: Early Game Strategy: As a Roamer, support your Mid Laner and Jungler by securing vision. Use Skill 2 to annoy enemies while aggressively checking bushes to ensure your team can farm safely.
strategy.midGame: Mid Game Strategy: Utilize your mobility to roam the entire map and cover areas where your allies might get ganked. It's crucial to have the macro awareness to track enemy CC (crowd control) skills and predict when your allies will be targeted.
strategy.lateGame: Late Game Strategy: Constantly watch out for enemies with strong CC skills. Your primary role is to stick to your Marksman or Mage and maintain an environment where they can freely deal damage.
strategy.teamfight: Teamfight Strategy: Absorb enemy attacks and aggro on the frontline. The moment the enemy uses strong AoE CC (like stuns or knockups), cast your Ultimate to cleanse your entire team and create an opportunity to counterattack.
strategy.commonMistakes: Common Beginner Mistakes: Panicking and casting your Ultimate when allies aren't CC'd or the CC isn't lethal, leaving your team defenseless against the enemy's truly dangerous CC.

synergy.reason: By releasing CC, we can continue to take advantage of her mobility and safely deliver continuous high firepower.
By removing the enemy's CC while his ULT is active, he can deal fatal area damage without interruption.

counters.reason: Because it doesn't rely on CC and attacks with overwhelming burst damage, the strength of CC removal cannot be utilized.
This is because continuous pokes and stuns from a distance will keep them from getting close, creating a situation where you have no choice but to use ULT.

passive.name: Passive Skill: Natural Will
passive.description:
[Tag] Purify, damage reduction, movement speed increase
Every 6 seconds, enters the "dream realm" for 2 seconds and recovers 40 HP (40 + 0.6% of additional HP). "Dream Realm": Removes CC from self and strengthens self for 2 seconds. While enhanced, all CC will be nullified, damage taken will be reduced by 15%, and movement speed will be increased by 15%.
[HP recovery] Lv.1: 40 / Lv.15: 80

skill1.description:
[Tag] Enhancement, movement speed reduction, damage
CD: 8 seconds / MP consumption: 60
Releases a phantom butterfly in a fan-shaped area and strengthens passive skills for 2 seconds. The butterfly deals 400 (400 + 72% of magic attack) magic damage to enemies within range and reduces enemy movement speed by 30% for 2 seconds.
[Basic damage] Lv.1: 400 / Lv.2: 480 / Lv.3: 560 / Lv.4: 640 / Lv.5: 720 / Lv.6: 800
[Movement speed reduction] Lv.1: 30% / Lv.2: 36% / Lv.3: 42% / Lv.4: 48% / Lv.5: 54% / Lv.6: 60%

skill2.description:

Deals 250 (250 + 40% of magic attack) magic damage to nearby enemies and applies 1 stack of engraving, and each stack deals 125 (125 + 20% of magic attack) magic damage to the target for 5 seconds. Maximum 5 stacks. At the same time, give 1 stack of Imprint to yourself and nearby friendly heroes, and each stack increases the target's movement speed by 3%. Maximum of 5 stacks, duration is 5 seconds. Passive effect: Activates skill 2 once every 7 seconds.

skill3.description:
[Tag] CC release, super armor, shield
CD: 60 seconds / MP consumption: 150
By deploying a dream realm, it removes CC from self and nearby allied heroes, and also grants a 700 (700 + 13% of additional HP) shield and CC immunity to self and allied heroes for 2 seconds.
[Cooldown] Lv.1: 60 / Lv.2: 55 / Lv.3: 50
[Granted Shield] Lv.1: 700 / Lv.2: 1050 / Lv.3: 1400

passive.name: magnetic barrier
passive.description: Skills can also deal the same amount of damage to towers and crystals, and CC skills can jam towers and crystals for 1.2 seconds. When you damage a tower or crystal with a skill, you will steal parts and recover 280 HP.

skill1.name: Small Overlord's Shield
skill1.description: Deploy your shield and attack. Gain a shield of 650 (650 + 16% of additional maximum HP) and increase your movement speed by 50% for 3 seconds (effect gradually decreases). At the same time, strengthens the next normal attack, jumps and slams the ground forcefully, dealing 569 (350 + 122% of physical attack power) physical damage to enemies within range and knocking them up for 1 second.

skill2.name: mechanical magic claw
skill2.description: Operate a mechanical panda and attack with magic claws within a fan-shaped area, dealing 580 (400 + 100% of physical attack power) physical damage to the enemy and stunning them for 1 second.

skill3.name: runaway panda
skill3.description:
Operate a mechanical panda and attack by spinning while spreading your arms for 3 seconds, dealing 380 (380 + 110% of additional physical attack power) physical damage to nearby enemies every 0.5 seconds. While spinning, reduce enemy movement speed by 15% for 0.5 seconds every second.
Other skills can be activated while spinning.

hero_016.status_text: Liu Zen Ryuzen Runaway Engine Adjustment Apprentice Support 0/100 Support/Tank Tactical Support Progress/CC Early Difficulty: Normal

strengths: Top-class tower siege performance that can damage and hinder defense towers with passives.
Has multiple CC skills (knockup/stun), making it easy to set up kills for allies.
weaknesses: It lacks excellent movement skills such as passing through walls, and lacks mobility and attack range.
If your own skills are on cooldown, you are likely to become a target for enemies and have few means of self-defense.
strategy.earlyGame: Early Game Strategy: Capitalize on the strong CC from Skill 1 and Skill 2 to aggressively invade the enemy jungle with your Jungler/Mid, or look for kills in lane. Make aggressive plays early on to expand your advantage.
strategy.midGame: Mid Game Strategy: Don't miss opportunities when your lane has a numbers advantage or the enemy recalls. Use your passive's tower interference ability to force tower dives, destroying towers and seizing map control.
strategy.lateGame: Late Game Strategy: Shift your role to peeling (protecting) your team's carries from enemy Assassins. Avoid unnecessary isolation, stick with your team, and reliably catch out any enemies who overstep with your skill combos.
strategy.teamfight: Teamfight Strategy: Stick to your carries and neutralize any diving enemies by landing your Skill 1 knockup and Skill 2 stun. When ahead, position yourself to block enemy escape routes, and during sieges, disable the tower to assist your allies in diving.
strategy.commonMistakes: Common Beginner Mistakes: Charging into the enemy team alone without your allies backing you up, only to get killed. Also, obsessing too much over destroying towers and failing to participate in teamfights.

synergy.reason: By continuing to hold down the enemy on the front lines, you can create an environment where you can continue to safely unleash ultra-high firepower from a distance.
Combined with his tower dive and CC, he can finish off enemies at once with continuous attacks.

counters.reason: Because it has high mobility, it can be destroyed by true damage from a distance before it can get close.
The acceleration from his skills makes it easier for the entire team to avoid his engagement, completely blocking his strengths.

passive.name: Lamentation
passive.description: Activate normal attacks and skills by playing. Every 3 times played, the next normal attack becomes an energy code and deals 290 (290 + 80% of magic attack) magic damage to enemies on the route.

skill1.name: Kyoka
skill1.description:
Plays a musical note, dealing 340 (340 + 47% of magic attack) magic damage to up to 2 enemies within range. Hit notes bounce back towards nearby enemies (up to 2 times).
If multiple notes hit the same hero target, the damage dealt by subsequent notes is reduced to 30%.

skill2.name: Rika
skill2.description:
Moves in the specified direction and plays the guitar, dealing 450 (450 + 60% of magic attack) magic damage to nearby enemies and reducing movement speed by 15% for 2 seconds. If you hit a hero, you will recover 100 (100 + 15% of magic attack) HP. When hitting a non-hero unit, the HP recovery effect is halved. While moving, damage reduction effect increases by 10% and movement speed reduction is nullified.
If you activate "Kyouka" while moving, you can move again.

skill3.name: Magical sound attack
skill3.description:
Plays violently for 5 seconds, dealing 200 (200 + 20% of magic attack) magic damage every 0.5 seconds.
Movement speed increases by 50% at the beginning of the performance, and decreases continuously over 3 seconds. If there are enemy heroes nearby during the performance, you will receive a 10% damage reduction effect, and for each additional enemy you will receive an additional 5% damage reduction effect.

strategy.earlyGame: Early Game Strategy: Use Skill 1 in the Mid Lane to safely clear minions. Your combat power and range are low in the early game, so focus on dodging enemy poke and farming safely until Level 4 without forcing fights.
strategy.midGame: Mid Game Strategy: You truly shine in skirmishes once you unlock your Ultimate and build Spell Vamp items. Roam to the sidelanes with your Jungler to gank, using your Ultimate to secure kills and build an advantage.
strategy.lateGame: Late Game Strategy: Your destructive power in teamfights peaks. Charging in head-on will get you focus-fired, so wait for your Tank to bait out the enemy's key CC (crowd control) skills, or look for an opening from the enemy's blind spot to engage.
strategy.teamfight: Teamfight Strategy: Utilize Summoner Spells like Flash to dive directly into the center of the enemy backline (Marksmen and Mages). Cast your Ultimate and wreak havoc in the enemy formation, wiping them out with overwhelming AoE damage and self-healing.
strategy.commonMistakes: Common Beginner Mistakes: Diving into the enemy team all alone before your allies engage, getting chain CC'd, and dying instantly before you can utilize your Ultimate's healing.

synergy.reason: Jump into a group of enemies and force them together, making it easy to hit with your ultimate.
Increased movement speed and recovery can support approaching enemies and survival.

counters.reason: A powerful suppression skill can completely stop your actions during ultimate.
Since he also has the suppression skill, his movements are blocked when he approaches.

passive.name: kiss of death
passive.description: Attacking an enemy from the front will not result in a critical hit, but attacking from behind will always result in a critical hit. Initial critical damage is 125%, and critical damage increases by 0.5% for every 1% critical rate. Attacking non-hero units head-on increases damage by 20%, up to a maximum of 2000 damage when attacking non-hero units.

skill1.name: arc light
skill1.description: Attacks twice with double blade, dealing 150 (150 + 65% of additional physical attack) physical damage with each attack.

skill2.name: quick feet
skill2.description:
Teleports forward and attacks one enemy near the destination, dealing 270 (270 + 85% of additional physical attack) physical damage and imprinting a mark on the enemy for 10 seconds. Additionally, own attack speed increases by 40% for 3 seconds.
Each time you attack a stamped enemy, reduce the target's movement speed by 25% for 1 second and reduce the cooldown of "Swift" by 1 second. Damage taken from enemies with engravings is reduced by 30%.
You can only mark one enemy at a time.

skill3.name: illusion dance
skill3.description:
After a short preparation time, enter stealth for 4 seconds. While in stealth, recovers 150 HP (150 + 25% of additional physical attack + 5% of additional HP) every 0.5 seconds and increases movement speed by 40%.
Attacking breaks stealth, increases your physical attack by 120 for 3 seconds, and increases your movement speed by 30% for 1 second each time you damage an enemy.
Passive: Defeating or assisting a hero resets all skill cooldowns.

High surprise attack performance and escape power with ultimate that allows you to enter a stealth state

Attacks from the front greatly reduce firepower, so careful positioning is required.
It's easy to get isolated in situations where there are multiple healthy enemies or if you can't get the first kill.
strategy.earlyGame: Early Game Strategy: Play as the Jungler and safely clear both sides of your jungle to hit Level 4 as fast as possible. You are very squishy early on, so if the enemy invades your jungle, wait for allies to rotate or prioritize safe farming.
strategy.midGame: Mid Game Strategy: Use your Ultimate's stealth to gank the lanes. Always attack enemies from behind to trigger guaranteed critical strikes, securing kills and aiming to snowball the game.
strategy.lateGame: Late Game Strategy: As enemies start to group up, it becomes harder to find isolated targets. Hide in the brush out of enemy vision and wait patiently for the perfect moment when the teamfight breaks out.
strategy.teamfight: Teamfight Strategy: Never be the one to initiate a fight. Wait until your allies engage, enemy CC and defensive skills are blown, and low-HP targets appear. Then, sneak up to their backline using stealth and aim for a kill streak (cooldown resets).
strategy.commonMistakes: Common Beginner Mistakes: Charging at enemies head-on or diving into a group of full-HP enemies alone. You won't benefit from the backstab damage bonus and will quickly be killed in return.

synergy.reason: Powerful controls stop the enemy's movement, making it easier to target attacks from behind.
It collects enemies and lowers their defense power, so it's easy to aim for skill reset by consecutive kills.

counters.reason: Silence skill and high durability can withstand burst damage and hinder combos.
It can withstand attacks due to its high durability and shield, and there is a high risk of being defeated by a counterattack.

passive.name: stone strike
passive.description:
Every time you deal damage with a normal attack or skill, you have a 50% chance to petrify the enemy for 0.75 seconds. Petrifying the same target has an 8 second cooldown.
Summons the power of the earth and gains a 300 (300 + 8% of additional HP) shield (resets every 3 seconds).

skill1.name: hurricane shock
skill1.description:
Charges in the specified direction and strengthens the next normal attack for 4 seconds. Enhanced normal attack deals 250 (250 + 50% of additional physical attack) physical damage to enemies within range and reduces enemy movement speed by 25% for 2 seconds.
If skill 1 is activated while skill 3 is activated, the charging speed will be slightly slower.

skill2.name: shock impact
skill2.description: Slams the hammer hard at the specified location, dealing 700 (700 + 200% of additional physical attack) physical damage to enemies within the area. Deals 50% additional damage to enemies in the center of the area.

skill3.name: storm hammer
skill3.description:
Swings the hammer for 3.2 seconds, increasing movement speed by 10%. Deals 300 (300 + 95% of additional physical attack) physical damage to nearby enemies every 0.4 seconds, and deals 50% additional damage to enemies in the outer area.
While swinging the hammer, your movement speed increases by 5% every 0.4 seconds, and you also gain a shield of 70 (70 + 3% of your additional HP), up to a maximum of 4 stacks.

strengths: Powerful movement obstruction (CC) with a passive that has a chance of petrifying the enemy.
High instantaneous firepower from skill combos, making it easy to gain an advantage in early lane battles and small group battles.
In group battles, his ultimate's wide range attacks and petrification have the potential to completely change the battle situation.
weaknesses: Has low mobility and is easily kited by long-range attacks or enemies with high mobility.
Since the activation of petrification is dependent on probability, there is instability in which CC cannot be determined in important situations.
In the latter half of the game, the influence tends to decrease as the enemy's durability and cooperation increase.
strategy.earlyGame: Early Game Strategy: In the Clash Lane, leverage your high base damage to aggressively trade with your opponent. Landing the center of your Skill 2 deals massive damage, making it easy to seize lane priority.
strategy.midGame: Mid Game Strategy: After pushing your lane, actively secure vision in the jungle or roam to the Mid Lane to participate in skirmishes. Hide in the brush and launch surprise skill combos to secure kills.
strategy.lateGame: Late Game Strategy: Enemy durability and mobility increase, making solo kills difficult. Shift your mindset to acting as a secondary tank, protecting your carries or checking the enemy carries, and coordinate closely with your team.
strategy.teamfight: Teamfight Strategy: Move to split the enemy frontline and backline, or use Flash to dive straight into the enemy backline. Spin your Ultimate to scatter your petrification passive over a wide area, creating a favorable battlefield for your allies.
strategy.commonMistakes: Common Beginner Mistakes: Missing Skill 2 because of its long cast animation by failing to predict enemy movement. Also, recklessly diving in the late game with a full damage build and getting killed before the petrification triggers.

synergy.reason: Combined with Kanematsu's powerful control, you can deliver reliable burst damage.
By blocking and controlling enemy attacks on the front lines, you can safely deal continuous damage.

counters.reason: Because it has high mobility and can poke from a long distance, it is difficult for Kane Mutsuya, who mainly attacks from short range, to fight.
It has powerful poke and control from a distance, allowing you to deal damage before you can get close.

passive.name: hourglass of time
passive.description: Each time you activate a skill, your movement speed increases by 15% to 30% for 2 seconds.

skill1.name: space-time bomb

After 3 seconds or when the target with the bomb dies, it deals 575 (575 + 60% of magic attack) magic damage to enemies within the area and reduces their movement speed by 15% for 2 seconds.
Defusing the bomb will cause an immediate explosion.

skill2.name: wave of time
skill2.description:
Channel the power of time and space to increase the movement speed of yourself and nearby allied heroes by 30% for 2 seconds. After the duration ends, recovers HP equal to 25% of the damage received while increasing movement speed.
Passive effect: Reduces skill cooldown by 7.5%.

skill3.name: flow of time
skill3.description:
Fires a reinforced bomb in the specified direction. When you hit an enemy or reach the maximum distance, you will develop a field that deals 500 (500 + 45% of magic attack) magic damage to enemies within the area and greatly reduces their movement speed for 1 second. When activated by interrupting an enemy's skill, it also applies a silence effect for 1 second.
After 5 seconds, the field will explode, dealing 1500 (1500 + 135% of magic attack) magic damage to enemies within the area.

strategy.midGame: Mid Game Strategy: Always stick with your allies and utilize your Skill 2's movement speed boost and HP recovery to turn skirmishes in your favor. Make quick decisions on whether to engage or retreat to control your team's pacing.
strategy.lateGame: Late Game Strategy: Once you complete cooldown reduction items, you can spam Skill 2 frequently. Constantly boost your team's mobility to prevent enemies from catching out isolated allies, and organize your formation for map objective fights.
strategy.teamfight: Teamfight Strategy: Use Skill 2 to accelerate your entire team into favorable positions. The moment enemies try to use their key skills, deploy your Ultimate to inflict an AoE silence. Interrupt their combos and set up the counterattack for your team.
strategy.commonMistakes: Common Beginner Mistakes: Wasting Skill 2 merely for getting around, only for it to be on cooldown when actual burst damage comes in, leaving you unable to protect your allies. Using it right before taking damage is the most critical point.

synergy.reason: With increased mobility and silence skills, you can move around safely and deal damage.
Speed ​​up and recovery can increase the survivability of less mobile Marksmen.

counters.reason: Possessing high mobility and burst damage, he can instantly defeat Sun Ping, who has low durability.
He has excellent poke and mobility, and can continuously apply pressure while avoiding Sun Ying's skills.

hero_021.sub_role: Area attack type Mage / continuous attack / recovery

passive.name: Passive Skill: Dark Medicine
passive.description: Normal attacks and skill hits will inflict a poison mark on the enemy (up to 5 stacks), dealing 20 (20 + 5% of magic attack) magic damage per second for 7 seconds. The healing skill gives you and allied heroes a mark of healing for 7 seconds (maximum of 5 stacks), and recovers the HP of allied heroes by 3 (3 + 2% of magic attack) and yourself by 3 (3 + 2% of magic attack + 0.15% of additional HP) every second.

skill1.name: Skill 1: Elixir of Death
skill1.description: Throws a vial containing deadly poison. When the vial falls to the ground and explodes, the poison will flow out for 4 seconds. Poison deals 65 (65 + 14% magic attack) magic damage per second to enemies within the area and reduces movement speed by 30% for 2 seconds. Poison can be produced once every 12.5 seconds (up to 3 stacks).

skill2.name: Skill 2: Diagnosis of right and wrong
skill2.description: Throws a medicine bottle, dealing 130 (130 + 30% of magic attack) magic damage to the enemy it hits, while also recovering 65 (65 + 15% of magic attack) HP of allied heroes around him.

skill3.name: Skill 3: Ruler of Life
skill3.description: Explodes all nearby engravings, dealing 150 (150 + 40% of magic attack) magic damage to enemies and restoring 70 (70 + 20% of magic attack) HP to allied heroes. Each stack increases the additional damage and recovery effect by 50%. Doctor of Light and Darkness recovers an additional 400 HP (400 + 30% of magic attack + 5% of additional HP).

strengths: A unique skill set that can deal continuous damage (poison) to enemies and heal allies at the same time.
The longer the battle goes on, the more poison stacks accumulate, and the power of the ultimate's total recovery and large damage increases.
Depending on the build, you can build up magic defense and physical defense, and demonstrate amazing tenacity as a sub-tank and healer.
weaknesses: The firing range is short, and you need to move forward to apply the skill, so you are likely to get hit.
Since he does not have movement skills (blink) or hard CC, he cannot escape if he is suddenly approached by a highly mobile Assassin.
The instantaneous burst damage is low, and if the composition and coordination of your allies is insufficient, you will be defeated before the poison is fully distributed.
strategy.earlyGame: Early Game Strategy: In the Mid Lane, weave basic attacks and skills to stack poison on enemies while winning damage trades. Your healing skills grant you high sustain during early laning, allowing you to aggressively push the minion wave.
strategy.midGame: Mid Game Strategy: Group up with your allies, using Skill 2 and basic attacks to stack poison on enemies and healing stacks on your team. In skirmishes, your sustained healing and damage make you stronger the longer the fight drags on.
strategy.lateGame: Late Game Strategy: Enemy burst damage becomes very high, so positioning requires extreme caution. Position yourself behind your frontline, maintaining healing stacks on your team while continuing to spread poison from a safe distance.
strategy.teamfight: Teamfight Strategy: Position yourself to hit both allies and enemies with your skills to build up as many stacks (max 5) as possible. Trigger your Ultimate when stacks are maxed out or when your allies' HP is critically low for a massive burst of healing and damage.
strategy.commonMistakes: Common Beginner Mistakes: Getting so focused on stacking poison that you step too far forward due to your short range, becoming isolated and burst down before you can cast your Ultimate.

synergy.reason: Tanks with high durability fight on the front lines, making it easier to accumulate poison stacks and heal allies.
Since you can block enemy attacks, you can create an environment that provides sustained recovery and damage.

counters.reason: It is difficult to carry into sustained battles because it inhibits recovery and deals burst damage.
It has high mobility and burst damage, and will defeat you without giving you a chance to recover.

passive.name: Passive: Reaper's Revenge
passive.description: Gains Shadow Power upon dealing or taking damage, increasing Movement Speed and healing effects (up to 70 Movement Speed and 80% increased healing).

skill1.name: Skill 1: Blood Echo
skill1.description: (Can be charged) Spins his scythe, splashing blood in all directions, dealing 252 (180 + 40% Physical Attack) physical damage to enemies and recovering 90 (90 + 2% extra HP) Health. Passive: When taking damage, has a 20% chance to cast Blood Echo. Damage and healing increase with charge time (up to 3 times. Effect halved if hitting non-hero units).

skill2.name: Skill 2: Deathscythe
skill2.description: Summons a scythe that retracts after 1.2s, pulling enemies in its path, dealing 325 (325 + 120% extra Physical Attack) magical damage, and slowing them by 25% for 2.5s. Passive: Deals 30% extra damage to targets below 30% HP.

skill3.name: Skill 3: Lay Waste
skill3.description: Charges Shadow Power to max and leaps to the target location, dealing 481 (300 + 100% Physical Attack) magical damage and taunting enemies. Base taunt duration is 1–1.5s, increasing by 0.2s for every 1000 extra HP (up to 2.5s).

strengths: Incredible durability in extended fights thanks to high healing and movement speed from his passive.
Powerful initiation capability with his AoE taunt ultimate (up to 2.5s), allowing him to dive and disrupt enemy formations.
Becomes an 'unsinkable' tank from mid-game onwards once fully equipped, absorbing massive damage.
weaknesses: Highly reliant on healing, making him extremely vulnerable to Anti-Heal items.
Low mobility and lack of escape skills mean he can be easily isolated and killed if his team does not follow up his initiation.
Weak against true damage, percentage-based damage, and heroes who can cleanse taunts.
strategy.earlyGame: Early Game Strategy: Farm steadily in the Clash Lane. Utilize your Skill 1's AoE damage and healing to clear minions, avoid forced fights, and prioritize farming gold and experience to rush Level 4.
strategy.midGame: Mid Game Strategy: Once you start completing defensive items, you gain overwhelming durability. Group up with your team and use your Ultimate's taunt to catch enemies, leading your team to secure kills, tower sieges, and Tyrants.
strategy.lateGame: Late Game Strategy: Stand at the very frontline of your team as the primary initiator. Always keep track of the enemy backline's (Marksmen and Mages) positions, and maintain a position where you can dive them the moment they show an opening.
strategy.teamfight: Teamfight Strategy: When the enemy carries are clumped up or your Assassins are ready to follow up, combine Flash with your Ultimate to dive in and taunt a large group of enemies to neutralize them. Wreak havoc in the enemy formation while healing with Skill 1.
strategy.commonMistakes: Common Beginner Mistakes: Initiating with your Ultimate alone when your allies are not following up or are too far away, successfully taunting the enemy but dying uselessly because no one can follow up with damage.

synergy.reason: She can unleash AoE and true damage while enemies are grouped by Bai Qi's taunt.
Her shields and support further enhance Bai Qi's survivability when taking damage in the middle of team fights.

counters.reason: His true damage ignores Bai Qi's high physical defense, dealing massive damage.
His long-range poke and crowd control prevent Bai Qi from getting close to land his taunt.

hero_023.sub_role: AoE Mage / Sustained Damage/Recovery / Early Game

passive.name: Passive: Blood of Eternity
passive.description: Casting a skill summons a shadow crow. Crows automatically attack nearby enemies, dealing 50 (50 + 7% Magical Attack) magical damage per hit, with an extra 80 (80 + 14% Magical Attack) magical damage to minions (halved against monsters). Also restores 20 (20 + 0.5% extra HP) Health. Summoning a crow grants 30 Shadow Power, increasing Movement Speed and healing. Movement Speed can increase by up to 30, and healing by up to 10%. Up to 6 crows can be summoned simultaneously. Crows disappear when Shadow Power drops to 0.

skill1.name: Skill 1: Abyssal Shift
skill1.description: Fires a shadow orb that deals 200 (200 + 24% Magical Attack) magical damage to enemies in its path. Casting this skill again makes Mi Yue descend at the orb's location, dealing 200 (200 + 24% Magical Attack) magical damage to nearby enemies. If the descent hits an enemy, she summons 1 shadow crow.

skill2.name: Skill 2: Ravenous Tether
skill2.description: Fires a shadow chain that tethers to the first enemy hit for up to 4s. Stacks a 1.25%–2.5% (scales with skill level) slow effect every 0.5s, absorbing 10 Physical Attack and 30 Magical Attack. Also deals 30 (30 + 7% Magical Attack) magical damage and heals 10 (10 + 0.25% extra HP). The chain breaks if they are over 1000 units apart. Tethering for 4s summons 1 shadow crow. Passive: Basic attacks are enhanced, dealing 200 (40 + 100% Physical Attack + 20% Magical Attack) magical damage and slowing the target by 10%, while granting 10 Shadow Power.

skill3.name: Skill 3: Umbral Moon
skill3.description: Fades into the shadows, cleansing all debuffs. Increases Movement Speed by 30% and becomes immune to all effects, then descends again after 2s. Deals 380 (380 + 70% Magical Attack) magical damage to nearby enemies when fading into shadows and when descending. If the descent hits an enemy, summons 1 shadow crow.

strengths: Incredible 1v1 dueling potential thanks to her Skill 2 attack absorption and self-healing.
Can stay in lane indefinitely without recalling due to sustained damage and healing from her passive shadow crows.
Her Ultimate grants untargetability and invincibility, allowing easy gank escapes or tower dives.
weaknesses: Lacks large-scale CC or burst damage, reducing her impact in large team fights.
Highly reliant on her skills; missing her Skill 2 tether drastically drops her damage and healing.
Vulnerable to hard CC; getting chain-CCed before casting her Ultimate usually results in a quick death.
strategy.earlyGame: Early Game Strategy: In the Clash Lane or Jungle, hit enemies or minions with your Skill 2 link to steal their stats, and seize lane priority with your overwhelming dueling power. Maintain your passive ravens and trade aggressively.
strategy.midGame: Mid Game Strategy: Capitalize on your strong 1v1 potential by relentlessly split pushing (pushing sidelanes solo). Invade the enemy jungle and draw the attention of multiple enemies to create advantageous situations for your team on the opposite side of the map.
strategy.lateGame: Late Game Strategy: Instead of joining teamfights, continue to apply relentless pressure on the sidelanes. If enemies come to defend, use your Ultimate's invincibility to escape safely, draining their time and disrupting their formation.
strategy.teamfight: Teamfight Strategy: If you must participate in a teamfight, do not engage from the front; flank from the side or behind. Connect your Skill 2 to the enemy carry to reduce their damage, and if focused, instantly use your Ultimate to drop aggro and survive tenaciously.
strategy.commonMistakes: Common Beginner Mistakes: Playing aggressively while your Skill 2 (chain) misses, resulting in a lack of both healing and damage, and getting killed in return. The golden rule is to back off obediently if Skill 2 misses.

synergy.reason: Provides control to lock down enemies, making it much easier for Mi Yue to land her tether (Skill 2).
Draws enemy aggro on the front line, allowing Mi Yue to safely approach to absorb stats and deal damage.

counters.reason: Possesses high durability and burst damage, farming fast enough to counter Mi Yue's pressure.
Highly mobile and can poke from a distance, making it very difficult for Mi Yue to close the gap and land her tether.

passive.name: Passive: Bloodline of the Beast
passive.description: Hitting an enemy hero with a halberd skill enchants the weapon for 8s. While enchanted, his basic attacks and skills deal a certain amount of true damage. If not enchanted, basic attacks and skills grant 30% Physical Pierce. Every basic attack on an enemy while enchanted restores 80 (80 + 1.3% extra Physical Attack) Health.

skill1.name: Skill 1: Impaling Thrust
skill1.description: Swings his halberd in the target direction, dealing 570 (570 + 220% extra Physical Attack) physical damage to enemies in range. If it does not hit an enemy hero, the halberd loses its enchanted state. While enchanted, each hit on an enemy hero heals him for 130 (130 + 2.5% extra Physical Attack) HP.

skill2.name: Skill 2: Trembling Grasp
skill2.description: Absorbs the souls of enemies in the target area, dealing 100 (100 + 35% extra Physical Attack) physical damage and slowing them by 15% for 3s. Gains a shield stack for each soul absorbed, up to 3 stacks. Each stack grants a 350 (350 + 7% extra HP) shield. 25% of the shield converts into HP every second at a ratio of 2 Shield = 1 HP.

skill3.name: Skill 3: Tremble, Mortals
skill3.description: Descends to the target location, deploying a killing zone and knocking up enemies in range for 1s. Immune to CC while descending. Upon landing, swings his halberd, dealing 650 (650 + 240% extra Physical Attack) physical damage to enemies in range. If enchanted, heals for 130 (130 + 2.5% extra Physical Attack) HP for each enemy hero hit. While inside the zone, gains 150 Physical Defense and 150 Magical Defense. Enemies touching the edge take 150 (150 + 75% extra Physical Attack) physical damage and are slowed by 30% for 2s. If the target is a hero, Lu Bu heals additional HP.

strengths: Can deal massive damage even against tanks thanks to his True Damage that completely ignores enemy defenses.
High survivability in team fights with his shield generation and HP recovery skills.
Provides powerful initiation and formation disruption with his wide-range jump and knock-up ultimate.
weaknesses: Very weak early game due to poor laning and wave clear abilities until he builds his core items.
Low mobility makes him vulnerable to kiting by ranged attackers and highly mobile heroes.
Blind-picking him carries the risk of facing strong 1v1 counter heroes that can dominate the lane.
strategy.earlyGame: Early Game Strategy: In the Clash Lane, land your Skill 1 on the enemy to maintain your enchanted state while prioritizing safe farming. You are very squishy early on, so be wary of ganks and safely clear minions under your tower without forcing fights.
strategy.midGame: Mid Game Strategy: Once your core items (defense or attack items) are completed, you become a force in skirmishes. Group with your team, use your Ultimate to engage, and leverage your true damage to overwhelm the enemy frontline.
strategy.lateGame: Late Game Strategy: As a top-tier Fighter, you apply immense pressure on the entire enemy team. Stay behind your frontline and dominate the battlefield by engaging when the enemy initiates or when their carries are clumped together.
strategy.teamfight: Teamfight Strategy: Do not dive blindly. Follow up on your team's CC by diving the enemy backline with your Ultimate, or land it to split the enemy formation. Hit multiple enemies with Skill 1 to sweep through them while sustaining yourself with true damage and healing.
strategy.commonMistakes: Common Beginner Mistakes: Continuing to trade basic attacks when your enchanted state (weapon buff) has expired and you've missed Skill 1. Lu Bu's damage is low without his enchant, so you must keep your distance until your Skill 1 hits.

synergy.reason: She can reset his Ultimate's cooldown, allowing him to jump twice and dominate team fights.
Pulls enemies together, ensuring Lu Bu's devastating AoE Ultimate hits multiple targets.

counters.reason: Uses his high mobility to dodge Lu Bu's attacks while safely poking him with true damage from afar.
Can use his skills to knock Lu Bu away the moment he lands from his Ultimate, preventing him from disrupting the formation.

passive.name: Passive: Blaze
passive.description: Basic attacks and skills apply ignite marks to enemies and enemy towers/crystals for 4s. At 4 stacks, the target ignites, taking 125 (125 + 17% Magical Attack) magical damage per second and being slowed by 15% for 1s. Ignited targets cannot be marked again.

skill1.name: Skill 1: Wildfire
skill1.description: Summons a wind domain that lasts for 3s, increasing the movement speed of allied heroes within by 5% (up to 5 heroes, doubled for himself) and slowing enemy heroes by 10%. Upon summoning, knocks back nearby enemies in the target direction, dealing 200 (200 + 30% Magical Attack) magical damage. If a fire domain is covered by the wind domain for more than 1s, the fire will spread in the direction of the wind to form new fire domains. The damage of the new and old fire domains can overlap. Fire domains deal 100 (100 + 25% Magical Attack) magical damage to enemies every 0.5s.

skill2.name: Skill 2: Scorching Bolt
skill2.description: Fires a scorching bolt at the target location, dealing 250 (250 + 40% Magical Attack) magical damage and leaving a fire domain that lasts for 6.5s. Deals 100 (100 + 30% Magical Attack) magical damage every 0.5s to enemies within the domain. If more than 6 fire domains are created, the oldest ones will disappear first. One scorching bolt is stacked every 9s, up to a maximum of 3 stacks (affected by cooldown reduction). Passive: If Zhou Yu is within a fire domain, he recovers 35 (35 + 7% Magical Attack + 2% extra Max HP) Health every 0.5s.

skill3.name: Skill 3: Flames of War
skill3.description: Unleashes a torrent of fire in the target direction, dealing 200 (200 + 40% Magical Attack) magical damage. If the fire passes through a fire domain, it creates a powerful gale, dealing 200 (200 + 40% Magical Attack) magical damage and stunning enemies for 1s.

strengths: Provides sustained AoE damage and powerful zone control with his fire domains.
Deals significant damage to enemy towers and objectives, granting strong lane suppression.
His passive healing while standing in fire domains gives him excellent durability and sustain in lane.
weaknesses: Low mobility and lack of movement skills make him very vulnerable to dives from high-mobility assassins.
Since self-healing is one of his strengths, his effectiveness can be heavily limited by enemy anti-heal items.
strategy.earlyGame: Early Game Strategy: Your strength lies in fast wave clear by combining Skill 2's fire and Skill 1's wind. Since it's easy to gain Mid Lane priority, push the wave and then actively coordinate with your Jungler and Roamer to gank sidelanes or secure jungle vision.
strategy.midGame: Mid Game Strategy: You exhibit unmatched strength in tower sieges and defenses. Spread fire under the enemy tower to apply pressure and whittle down the tower from a safe position. In skirmishes around the Tyrant or Overlord, cover narrow chokepoints with fire to effectively block enemy entry.
strategy.lateGame: Late Game Strategy: You play the role of controlling the teamfight formation. Spread fire in terrain where enemies tend to clump up, restricting their movement while dealing continuous damage. Since your durability is low, always cast your skills from behind your allies and be mindful of your positioning to guard against Assassin ambushes.
strategy.teamfight: Teamfight Strategy: First, place fire with Skill 2, then use Skill 1 to spread the fire toward the enemy team. The ideal combo is to hit enemies standing on the fire with Skill 3 (Ultimate) to stun them. Rather than forcing kills, focus on spreading fire over a wide area to divide the enemy team and create a favorable 'zone' for your allies to fight.
strategy.commonMistakes: Common Beginner Mistakes: Beginners often cast Skill 1's wind in a random direction, failing to spread the fire where they want it. Since you can specify the wind's direction, practice spreading fire accurately toward enemy escape routes or towers. Also, be careful not to step too far forward and get easily killed by enemy Assassins due to poor positioning.

synergy.reason: Zhong Kui can hook enemies directly into Zhou Yu's fire domains, ensuring they take massive sustained damage.
Can protect Zhou Yu with shields and a teleport when targeted by assassins, ensuring he survives to continue outputting damage.

counters.reason: High mobility allows him to easily bypass the fire domains and quickly burst down Zhou Yu.
His long-range poke and stuns make it very difficult for Zhou Yu to set up his fire formations.
